name: CPPORTAL
tagline: A Congestion Pricing Portal for real-time analysis of air quality impacts from New York City's congestion pricing policy.
body: Collecting and integrating a large body of public transit and air quality data, then building the analytical pipeline to ask whether the cordon actually moved the needle on pollution. The portal stitches together monitor-day observations into a coherent panel and serves results in near-real-time.

name: MOVESAI
tagline: A machine-learning short-form surrogate of the EPA's MOVES vehicle emissions simulator — fast, queryable, and deployable.
body: MOVES is powerful but heavy. MOVESAI learns a compact surrogate model that reproduces its emissions outputs at a fraction of the runtime, wrapped in an API and front-end so others can actually use it — then writes the whole thing up.

name: BIKESHARE
tagline: One giant bikeshare mobility database to rule them all.
body: An ambitious effort to fetch, unify, and model bikeshare trip data at global scale — treating riders' movements as networks and asking what patterns emerge once cities can be compared on common ground.
question: What could we learn if we could see how bikeshare users travel in cities across the world?

name: Climate Action in Transportation (CAT)
tagline: A dashboard system that lets local decision-makers estimate transportation emissions online — without running MOVES locally.
body: Lead software developer & coordinator on a multi-million-dollar US-DOT-funded effort at Cornell with Dr. Oliver Gao. We build the dashboards, databases, APIs, and cloud jobs that turn EPA MOVES into a public-facing emissions tool: CAT VISUALIZER, policyoptimizer, catr, moveslite, calculator.

name: Social Infrastructure Mapping
tagline: Mapping community spaces that build social capital — across Boston, Fukuoka, and the 25 largest US cities.
body: With colleagues at Northeastern and MIT, mapping community-level places (parks, libraries, places of worship, social businesses) using the Google Maps Places API + ground-truthing, then linking to pandemic, mobility, and resilience outcomes. Now in expansion phase with Cornell undergraduates across US cities.

name: Evacuation with Mobility Data
tagline: Estimating evacuation from hurricanes, wildfires, earthquakes, and floods using anonymized cell-phone movement data.
body: Replacing roadside / post-hoc surveys with passive mobility data to measure who evacuates, where they go, and what predicts that flow — Hurricane Dorian, the 2018 Hokkaido earthquake, Hurricane Zeta, the Glass Fire, COVID-era evacuation, and more.

name: Polarization & Health
tagline: Two national surveys testing how partisan polarization shapes Americans' physical and mental well-being.
body: Drafted and administered two nationwide surveys on trust, polarization, and health. Findings: cross-party division is linked to worse health outcomes; close in-group ties buffer that effect; polarization also predicts COVID-19 vaccination uptake.
